item.title: Iran war weighs on global economy as IMF meeting starts
item.description: Alarm over the impact of the Iran war on the global economy grew on Monday with more countries announcing emergency support measures to combat rising energy costs, while others appealed for international ​support.

item.title: US existing home sales drop to nine-month low in March amid tight supply
item.description: U.S. existing home sales fell to a nine-month low in March amid tight inventory ​and growing concerns over the labor market, and a recent increase in mortgage rates because of the war with ‌Iran could limit activity this year.

item.title: Dutch regulator notifies European Commission of plan to seek EU approval for Tesla's FSD
item.description: The Dutch vehicle ​authority RDW ‌has notified the European ​Commission ​of its plan to ⁠seek ​European Union-wide ​approval for Tesla's Full-Self Driving ​system, Bernd ​van Nieuwenhoven, general ‌manager ⁠of type approvals at the ​regulator, ​told ⁠Reuters on Monday.

item.title: UK to probe Paramount-Warner Bros deal in coming weeks, competition watchdog says
item.description: Britain's antitrust watchdog is expected to launch an investigation into Paramount Skydance's planned $110 billion acquisition of ​Warner Bros Discovery in "the coming weeks," ‌a spokesperson said on Monday.

item.title: Roblox to introduce age-based accounts in child safety push
item.description: Videogame platform Roblox on Monday said it will create tailored accounts for young users in a push to shore ​up online safety, as it deals with intense global ‌scrutiny over concerns of child endangerment.

item.title: Tesco partners with Adobe to ramp up AI‑driven personalised marketing
item.description: Tesco, Britain's biggest food retailer, has partnered with U.S. software group Adobe to deepen its use of artificial intelligence ​in analysing customer data, aiming to boost sales through ‌more personalised marketing, the groups said on Monday.

item.title: OPEC lowers second-quarter global oil demand forecast on Iran war
item.description: OPEC on Monday lowered its forecast for world oil demand in the second quarter by 500,000 barrels per day, the producer group's monthly oil report showed in its first public assessment of the Iran war's impact ​on the market.

item.title: Bill Ackman kicks off roadshow for combined IPO of Pershing Square, new fund
item.description: Bill Ackman's Pershing Square kicked off a roadshow for the U.S. initial ​public offerings of the billionaire investor's management company and a new ‌fund on Monday.

item.title: Conagra Brands names J.M. Smucker executive John Brase as new CEO
item.description: Conagra Brands named J.M. Smucker ​executive John Brase as ‌its new CEO, succeeding Sean Connolly, who will step down after ​more than a decade ​at the helm of the ⁠packaged food maker.

item.title: Goldman Sachs posts higher profit on strength in dealmaking, equities trading
item.description: Goldman Sachs posted a rise in first-quarter profit on Monday, as the Wall Street giant benefited from strength in dealmaking and equities trading.

item.title: Social media platforms need to stop never-ending scrolling, UK's Starmer says
item.description: Social media platforms such as Instagram and TikTok need to take action to stop young users wasting hours ​mindlessly scrolling never-ending videos, British Prime Minister Keir ‌Starmer said on Monday.

item.title: REIT National Healthcare aims $1.1 billion in valuation in US IPO
item.description: National Healthcare Properties said on Monday it was targeting a valuation of about $1.1 billion in ​its U.S. initial public offering, the second real ‌estate investment trust to hit the new listings markets in recent weeks.

item.title: Morning Bid: Blockade takes its toll
item.description: What matters in U.S. and global markets today

item.title: Morgan Stanley maintains oil price forecasts and predicts slow recovery in supply
item.description: Morgan Stanley left its Brent crude oil price forecasts unchanged on Monday, at $110 a barrel for ​the second quarter of 2026 and $100 a ‌barrel in the third quarter, falling to $80 a barrel in 2027.

item.title: Hedge funds rush into bullish stocks bets ahead of weekend US-Iran talks, Goldman note says
item.description: Hedge funds last week piled into bets that stocks would rise, on ​expectations of a ceasefire in Iran, said two ‌Goldman Sachs client notes on Saturday and seen by Reuters on Monday.

item.title: Traton truck deliveries fall 6% in first quarter as US weakness persists
item.description: German truck manufacturer Traton reported a 6% drop ​in vehicle sales for the first quarter of ‌2026 on Monday, driven by a 21% decline in its U.S.-based International Motors brand.

item.title: Wall Street futures drop after US-Iran peace talks fail
item.description: Wall ​Street futures slipped on Monday, starting the week on a subdued note, after ‌talks between the U.S. and Iran over the weekend failed to deliver a deal to end the war.

item.title: Volkswagen posts weak start to year as China, US demand slumps further
item.description: Volkswagen ​reported a dip in global deliveries ‌at the start of the year, falling 4% year on year in the first three ​months of 2026, as the German ​carmaker battles with weak demand in ⁠China and the United States.

item.title: Audi Q1 deliveries fall 6.1% on weak demand in China and North America
item.description: Volkswagen's premium brand Audi on Monday said its first-quarter deliveries fell 6.1%, ​weighed down by weaker demand in ‌China and North America.

item.title: Exclusive: Chilean copper miner Codelco, contractors fined after deadly mine collapse
item.description: Chilean state copper miner Codelco was fined by labor authorities after last year's deadly collapse at its El Teniente mine, while three contractors whose workers were killed or injured were sanctioned more heavily, according to inspection records obtained by Reuters through public-records requests.

item.title: China poised for Q1 GDP growth rebound but Iran war dims 2026 outlook: Reuters poll
item.description: China's economy likely regained some momentum in the first quarter on solid ‌exports, but growth is expected to cool over the rest of 2026 as the Middle East crisis threatens to choke corporate profits and sap overseas demand, a Reuters poll showed.

item.title: World Bank chief sounds alarm about looming jobs crisis even after war ends
item.description: The Middle East war will dominate global finance officials' talks this week in Washington, but World Bank President Ajay Banga is sounding ​the alarm about a bigger, looming crisis: a huge gap in jobs for the 1.2 billion people who will reach working age in developing ‌countries in the next 10 to 15 years.

item.title: Chinese AI startup StepFun to unwind offshore structure to pave way for IPO, sources say
item.description: Chinese AI agent StepFun is unwinding its offshore incorporation structure to pave the way for a planned Hong Kong initial public offering, three sources said, ​as Beijing tightens scrutiny of a system widely used for overseas fundraising.

item.title: Basic-Fit data breach exposes details of gym members in Netherlands, elsewhere
item.description: Gym operator Basic-Fit has suffered a data breach involving "active members ​in several countries" and ​affecting around 200,000 of its clients ⁠in the Netherlands alone, ​it said on Monday.

item.title: UK fintech firm Wise's quarterly cross-border volumes surge ahead of Nasdaq debut
item.description: British fintech group Wise said ‌on Monday cross-border transaction volumes rose 26% in the fourth quarter to 49.4 billion ​pounds ($66.2 billion), reinforcing its expectation that ​annual profit margins will land near ⁠the top end of its forecast ​range.

item.title: Baker Hughes sells Waygate unit to Hexagon for about $1.45 billion
item.description: Oilfield services provider Baker Hughes sold its Waygate Technologies unit ​to Swedish industrial technology group Hexagon ‌for about $1.45 billion in cash, the two companies said on Monday.

item.title: Exclusive: Luxury brands face profits squeeze as Iran conflict shrinks Dubai Mall sales
item.description: Sales at Europe's biggest luxury brands have shrunk in Dubai and Abu Dhabi as the Iran conflict hit the sector's fastest-growing market in the latest setback for the $400 billion industry ​whose value has contracted over the last three years.

item.title: China's exports set to lose momentum as Iran war undercuts AI-driven boom: Reuters poll
item.description: China's export engine likely slowed in March ​as buyers chasing an AI-fuelled future confront the hard reality of war in the Middle East, which ‌has sparked an energy shock and revived market anxiety of past Gulf conflicts.

item.title: Morning Bid: Oil surges on US blockade of Iran
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Tom Westbrook

item.title: TSMC likely to book fourth straight quarter of record profit on insatiable AI demand
item.description: TSMC, the world's largest manufacturer of advanced artificial intelligence chips, will likely notch ‌up a fourth consecutive quarter of record earnings with a 50% surge in net profit for January-March thanks to booming demand for AI infrastructure.
